key_findings.finding: Pure QLIKE loss (alpha=0.0) is the only factor that yields a meaningful improvement: QLIKE improves from -5.0809 to -5.0837, R² increases from 0.633 to 0.643, and systematic negative bias is essentially eliminated.
key_findings.recommendation: Switch production loss to pure QLIKE (alpha=0.0). Do not use MSE loss with log-domain targets.

key_findings.finding: HAR context does not improve quality: patch_encoder with and without HAR is statistically indistinguishable; decoder_only (no HAR by design) slightly improves R² and reduces bias.
key_findings.recommendation: Remove HAR context from the production pipeline; prefer simpler models without HAR.

key_findings.finding: Transformer architecture choice is not a bottleneck: patch_encoder/decoder_only/patch_decoder/vanilla_enc_dec are statistically similar on QLIKE (spread ~0.0068).
key_findings.recommendation: For production, prefer decoder_only as the simplest/most compact architecture with comparable QLIKE.

key_findings.finding: Seq_len, patch_size and model capacity changes stay within noise: increasing context up to 48h, patch_size across 1..48, and parameters up to 1.26M does not improve QLIKE meaningfully.
key_findings.recommendation: Keep seq_len=240; avoid scaling model size for this dataset; patch_size can stay at 12 (or 6 as a near-tie).

key_findings.finding: Feature composition matters: dropping volatility estimator features severely hurts (ΔQLIKE=+0.049, R² drops to ~0.41). Other feature groups are marginal; some (price_sma, volume) slightly improve QLIKE when removed.
key_findings.recommendation: Keep volatility estimator features. Consider removing price_sma and volume features for simplification.

key_findings.finding: Ensemble ablation results are not valid due to methodology: early walk-forward folds were evaluated far beyond their training window, causing distribution shift and degraded ensemble performance.
key_findings.recommendation: Redesign ensemble evaluation: train multiple models on the same window with different seeds and ensemble on a shared validation segment.
